cookies.title: Cookie settings
cookies.description: We use strictly necessary cookies to run the site and, with your consent, analytics and marketing cookies to improve the service. You can accept, reject or customise your choice. Read more in our
cookies.gdprNote: Complies with the EU GDPR and ePrivacy Directive.

necessary.title: Strictly necessary
necessary.desc: Required to sign in, keep your session and protect the site. No consent needed.

analytics.title: Analytics & performance
analytics.desc: Help us understand how the site is used so we can improve it. Only enabled with your consent.

marketing.title: Marketing
marketing.desc: Allow measuring campaigns and showing relevant content. Only enabled with your consent.

errors.notFoundDesc: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.
errors.boundaryDesc: Something went wrong on our end. You can try refreshing or head back home.

quick.title: Quick access
quick.uploadHint: Upload MVT files directly without using the wizard.

redeem.title: Buy credits
redeem.desc: Enter the credit token provided by the administrator.
redeem.label: Token code

purchase.title: Mobile Forensic Analysis
purchase.description: Technical verification of spyware, malware and suspicious activity indicators on mobile devices using artifacts, IOC/STIX2 indicators and MVT-compatible forensic tools.
purchase.consent: Analysis performed with the device owner's consent.
purchase.checkoutSuccess: Payment confirmed! Your credits will be added in a few seconds.

meta.title: Spyware Forensic Analyzer — Forensic analysis of spyware indicators
meta.description: Preliminary forensic analysis platform for mobile devices, powered by MVT (Mobile Verification Toolkit). Detects potential indicators of compromise.
meta.ogDescription: Forensic analysis of spyware indicators on mobile devices, powered by MVT.

hero.badge: Powered by MVT · Mobile Verification Toolkit
hero.subtitle: Upload the files generated by MVT or a prepared backup and get a structured report with potential indicators of compromise, dates, domains, and suspicious artifacts.
hero.disclaimer: This platform <0>does not install spyware</0>, does not access devices without permission, and does not perform surveillance. It provides technical indicators, not absolute certification of infection.

mvt.title: MVT engine
mvt.desc: Processes artifacts generated by Mobile Verification Toolkit, including STIX2/IOC results.

visual.title: Visual results
visual.desc: Metrics, timeline, severity, and source for every detected indicator.

report.title: Exportable report
report.desc: Executive summary and evidence in PDF, ready to share with your team.

privacy.title: Privacy by design
privacy.desc: Files encrypted at rest. Permanent deletion on demand.

normalized.title: Normalized indicators
normalized.desc: Domains, processes, paths, hashes, and events correlated with public feeds.

consent.title: Consent first
consent.desc: Designed for analysis with explicit permission from the device owner.

how.title: Three steps for a preliminary analysis

s1.d: Upload the JSON/CSV results exported by MVT or a backup prepared for analysis.

s2.d: The backend runs MVT in an isolated environment and correlates IOCs against public feeds.

s3.d: Receive matches, estimated risk level, timeline, and a downloadable PDF.

legal.title: Legal notice
legal.body: Spyware Forensic Analyzer is a preliminary forensic analysis tool. Results are technical indicators based on known patterns and do not constitute an absolute certification of infection. Analysis must be performed only with explicit consent from the device owner. We do not share data with third parties.

footer.engine: Technical engine: MVT — Mobile Verification Toolkit (Amnesty International)

login.sideDesc: Your files are processed in an isolated environment and can be permanently deleted at any time.
login.subtitle: Access your analyses and reports.
login.toggleToRegister: Don't have an account? Sign up
login.toggleToLogin: Already have an account? Log in
login.demoNote: Demo: any email and password signs you in. Real authentication will be wired to the backend.

empty.title: No analyses yet
empty.desc: Upload your first MVT artifact to get started.

step1.title: Which device do you want to analyze?
step1.subtitle: Pick the mobile operating system.

noCredits.title: You have no credits available
noCredits.body: You need at least 1 credit to start an analysis. Top up credits from your dashboard to enable the options.

step2.title: Which computer will you use?
step2.subtitle: You need a computer with a USB cable to connect the phone.

blocked.title: iPhone requires a Mac
blocked.body: To analyze an iPhone you need to make the backup from a Mac (or from Windows with iTunes and then process it on Mac/Linux). Switch computer to continue.

header.title: Follow the steps in order
header.subtitle: One step at a time. Don't skip any or the analysis won't work.

preamble.title: Get the cable and phone ready
preamble.body: Have a USB cable handy (preferably the original one, that transfers data and not just charges). During the whole process the phone must stay unlocked with the screen on. Don't plug anything in yet: first you'll download the app on the computer.

dev.title: Enable developer mode on the phone
dev.body: On the phone, open Settings → About phone and tap 7 times on "Build number". You'll see a message: "You are now a developer".
routes: Samsung: Settings → About phone → Software information → tap "Build number" 7 times.
Xiaomi/Redmi: Settings → About phone → tap "MIUI version" 7 times.
Pixel/Stock Android: Settings → About phone → tap "Build number" 7 times.
Huawei/Honor: Settings → System → About phone → tap "Build number" 7 times.

usb.title: Enable USB Debugging
usb.body: On the same phone, go to Settings → System → Developer options and turn on "USB Debugging". Confirm when prompted. Still don't plug in the cable yet.

connect.title: Now plug the phone into the computer via USB
connect.body: With both devices already isolated (airplane mode / no network), plug the phone into the computer with the USB cable. On the phone you'll see a prompt: "Allow USB debugging?". Tick "Always allow from this computer" and tap OK.

iosTrust.title: Plug in the iPhone and trust the computer
iosTrust.body: With both devices already in airplane mode, now plug the iPhone into the computer via USB. Unlock it and, when the "Trust this computer?" prompt appears, tap Trust and enter the iPhone passcode.

iosBackup.title: Create an encrypted iPhone backup
iosBackup.body: With the iPhone already plugged in, open Finder (macOS Catalina or later) or iTunes, select the iPhone, tick "Encrypt local backup" and set a password.
iosBackup.warn: ⚠ Write down the password: you'll need it later.

iosKeep.title: Keep the iPhone connected and unlocked
iosKeep.body: During the whole analysis the iPhone must stay connected via USB and unlocked. If it locks, unlock it again.

download.title: Download the desktop app
download.intro: While you still have Internet, download the MVT Insight Desktop app on your computer now. Later you'll plug in the phone via USB and the app will do the rest: download the tools, collect data from the phone, and save a ZIP ready to upload here. No black window, no commands.
download.version: Version 1.0 · Analysis with visual UI and progress bars
instructions: Unzip the downloaded file and double-click MvtInsight to open the app. Don't click "Start analysis" yet: first you have to isolate the devices and plug in the phone.

protocol.title: Isolate the devices before plugging in
protocol.intro: Before plugging the phone into the computer, isolate both from the network. That way, if there is spyware, it can't "notice" you're analyzing or leak data during the process:

a.title: Computer offline
a.body: Unplug the network cable and turn off the Wi-Fi on the computer (or put it in airplane mode). The app is already downloaded, so you don't need Internet from here on.

b.title: Phone in airplane mode
b.body: Enable airplane mode on the phone to cut its Internet. Don't plug it into USB yet: you'll do that in the next step. Airplane mode does not disable USB debugging, so the connection will still work.

run.title: Open the app and run the analysis
run.body: With the phone already connected via USB and unlocked, go back to the MVT Insight Desktop app and click "Start analysis". You'll see progress bars for each phase (downloading tools and collecting data from the phone). When it finishes, the app will tell you where the ZIP was saved.

upload.title: Upload the ZIP generated by the app
upload.body: The app will show an "Upload to report" button and the path to the file mvt-results-YYYYMMDD.zip (usually in Downloads). Click the button below to upload it and view the report.

step4.title: Upload the results ZIP
step4.subtitle: Drag the .zip generated by the script (or the loose .json files).

errors.unsupported: Unsupported file: {{name}}. Only .json or .zip generated by MVT.

step4.consent: I'm the device owner or I have explicit consent. Files are processed locally in my browser.
